content.seo_title: Build a Portfolio: Email Automation with Antigravity & Claude
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about no portfolio. Learn advanced email automation using Antigravity and Claude Code to build high-impact projects that land you jobs.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' problem, build a high-utility email automation system that solves a real business pain. By leveraging Antigravity for infrastructure and Claude Code for iterative development, you create a production-grade asset that demonstrates architectural depth, API orchestration, and professional-grade code quality to potential employers.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank GitHub profile is deafening. You've studied the tutorials, memorized the syntax, and watched the lectures, but when you look at your portfolio, you see nothing but empty repos. This isn't a failure of skill; it's a failure of narrative. You don't need another 'To-Do list' app; you need a system that breathes, operates, and delivers value.

Today, we bridge the gap between student and engineer. We are building an autonomous email orchestration engine. This project doesn't just show you can code; it shows you can solve business problems. Using Antigravity to handle the heavy lifting and Claude Code to accelerate your architectural velocity, you will build a project that makes recruiters stop scrolling.

steps.title: Define the Business Logic
steps.description: Before writing code, define the trigger, the data transformation, and the delivery mechanism. We are building a lead-nurture automation that parses incoming webhooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Systems Architect. Design a modular schema for an email automation service that handles incoming webhooks, validates JSON payloads, and queues jobs for processing.

steps.title: Establish the Infrastructure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the environment. This ensures your project is production-ready from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust project structure with environment variable management, error handling middleware, and a clean interface for integrating third-party email APIs.

steps.title: Integrate Email Orchestration
steps.description: Implement the core logic to send transactional emails. Focus on modularity so you can swap providers (SendGrid, Postmark) easily.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service layer for email delivery that includes retry logic, exponential backoff, and logging for failed delivery attempts.

steps.title: Implement Webhook Security
steps.description: Real-world apps require security. Validate incoming requests using HMAC signatures to prevent unauthorized access.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a security decorator for FastAPI that validates webhook signatures using a shared secret key stored in environment variables.

steps.title: Automate Testing and Deployment
steps.description: A portfolio project is useless if it's broken. Set up automated tests to ensure your automation remains stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit and integration tests using Pytest for the entire email automation flow, covering success, failure, and edge cases.

pro_tips: Always wrap your external API calls in a Circuit Breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures.
Use structured logging (JSON format) from day one; it makes debugging production issues 10x faster.
Document your architectural decisions in an ADR (Architecture Decision Record) folder inside your repo; it proves you think like a senior dev.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode for your email automation to allow testing without actually spamming real user inboxes.
Focus on the 'Observability' of your system—add a simple health-check endpoint that reports on the last 5 successful deliveries.
content.next_steps: Once your email automation is live, deploy it to a free-tier cloud provider like Railway or Render. Create a README.md that details the problem you solved, your architectural choices, and the metrics you tracked. Share this on LinkedIn with a demo video.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn 'no experience' into 'proven builder'. Don't just read this—execute it. Build the engine, document the process, and own your career trajectory. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
